components.title: Accordion (Drupal Paragraph Bundle)
avoidWhen: Content that should always be visible
components.agentPrompt: Enable the uswds_paragraph_components_accordion submodule. Add a Paragraphs field to your content type. Select 'USWDS Accordion' as an allowed bundle. Editors create accordion items as child paragraphs.

fieldDefinitions.field_uswds_accordion_item: Entity reference revisions to uswds_accordion_item paragraph type (unlimited)
fieldDefinitions.field_uswds_heading: Text field for accordion section title

editableAreas: Template override in theme: copy to themes/custom/mytheme/templates/paragraph--uswds-accordion.html.twig
Allowed bundles on the parent paragraph field
knownLimitations: Uses jQuery-based USWDS JS by default
Accordion items are child paragraphs — cannot reference existing nodes

components.title: Alert (Drupal Paragraph Bundle)
avoidWhen: Form validation errors (use Drupal's native form error handling)
components.agentPrompt: Enable the uswds_paragraph_components_alert submodule. Add a Paragraphs field allowing 'USWDS Alert' bundle. Editor selects alert type (info, success, warning, error) and enters body text.

fieldDefinitions.field_uswds_alert_type: List (text) with options: info, success, warning, error
fieldDefinitions.field_uswds_alert_body: Text formatted field for alert content

editableAreas: Template override for custom alert styling
preserveElements: usa-alert class and variant classes (usa-alert--success, etc.)
knownLimitations: For site-wide alerts, use Drupal's site_alert module instead
Alert type is fixed at creation time — no dynamic state changes

components.title: Banner (Drupal Theme Region)
useWhen: Required on all federal government websites
components.agentPrompt: The uswds base theme includes the government banner in the header_top region by default. It uses the usa-banner markup with accordion behavior. Override the template in your subtheme to customize.
knownLimitations: Banner is built into the theme — customizing requires template override
The until-found hidden pattern needs custom JS

components.title: Breadcrumb (Drupal Theme Region)
components.agentPrompt: The uswds base theme maps Drupal's breadcrumb to usa-breadcrumb markup. Place the system breadcrumb block in the breadcrumb region. The theme handles the Twig conversion from Drupal's breadcrumb trail to usa-breadcrumb__list items.
components.twigTemplate: breadcrumb.html.twig in themes/contrib/uswds/templates/. The base theme converts Drupal's breadcrumb variables into usa-breadcrumb__list structure.
editableAreas: Breadcrumb block placement (which pages show it)
knownLimitations: Drupal's breadcrumb builder determines the trail — theme just styles it
Wrap and naviprev-next variants need custom preprocess logic

components.title: Button (Drupal Custom Twig)
avoidWhen: When a link should look like a button (use usa-button class on <a>)
components.agentPrompt: No paragraph bundle exists for buttons. For form buttons, override form-element.html.twig or input.html.twig in your theme to add usa-button classes. For content buttons, use Twig Tweak's drupal_link() with usa-button class, or write a custom block plugin.
components.twigTemplate: input--submit.html.twig, form-element.html.twig, or field--field-link.html.twig in themes/custom/mytheme/templates/
components.preprocessFunction: mytheme_preprocess_input() — add usa-button class to submit inputs. mytheme_preprocess_link() — add usa-button to link buttons.
knownLimitations: Drupal's Form API generates inputs, not <button> elements by default — use #type => 'button' for <button> tag
Button variants must be added programmatically in preprocess

components.title: Card (Drupal Paragraph Bundle)
avoidWhen: When you need complex card interactions
components.agentPrompt: Enable uswds_paragraph_components_cards submodule. The card bundle includes sub-bundles: USWDS Cards (Regular) and USWDS Cards (Flag). Create a parent paragraph of type 'uswds_card_group' then add card children. Each card has image, heading, body, and optional link.
knownLimitations: Card group requires parent paragraph with child cards
Flag variant needs specific image dimensions
Breakpoint submodule needed for responsive grid

components.title: Data Visualizations (Drupal Field Type — plotly_js)
useWhen: Any data visualization on a Drupal government site
avoidWhen: When USWDS's native (limited) chart patterns suffice
components.agentPrompt: Install plotly_js module: composer require drupal/plotly_js. Run drush plotly_js:download-plotly to get the library. Add a 'Plotly.js Graph' field to your content type. Editors configure chart type and data via the field widget. Graph templates (YAML) control rendering.
components.twigTemplate: plotly-js-graph.html.twig in modules/contrib/plotly_js/templates/. Graph templates in graph_templates/ directory (YAML-based, overrideable).
components.preprocessFunction: plotly_js_preprocess_field() — prepare graph data for JS rendering
components.configYaml:
# Graph template cascade (most specific wins):
# PLOT_NAME.yml
# PLOT_NAME--ENTITY_TYPE.yml
# PLOT_NAME--ENTITY_TYPE--FIELD_MACHINE_NAME.yml
# PLOT_NAME--ENTITY_TYPE--ENTITY_ID.yml
# PLOT_NAME--ENTITY_TYPE--ENTITY_ID--FIELD_MACHINE_NAME.yml
components.scssSettings: Chart colors should inherit from USWDS theme tokens for visual consistency

fieldDefinitions.field_chart: Plotly.js Graph field type — stores chart type, data, and display settings
fieldDefinitions.chart_data: JSON or CSV data for the chart
fieldDefinitions.display_settings: Width, height, colors, legend, axis labels

dependencies: Plotly.js library (via drush download or remote)
editableAreas: Chart colors to match USWDS palette
preserveElements: Chart container div with unique ID
Accessibility: provide data table alternative for screen readers
knownLimitations: Requires Drupal 11.3+ or 12
Plotly.js is a large JS library (~3MB)
Not integrated with USWDS design tokens — colors must be set manually
Charts are JS-rendered (no server-side fallback)

components.title: Footer (Drupal Theme Region)
useWhen: Site-wide footer with navigation links and agency info
components.agentPrompt: The uswds base theme provides footer_menu, footer, and footer_secondary regions. Place a menu block in footer_menu for footer links. Place custom blocks in footer for agency info. Theme settings control slim vs big footer variant.
components.twigTemplate: page.html.twig — look for region-footer, region-footer_menu, region-footer_secondary. Override in your subtheme.
editableAreas: Footer variant (slim, big, default) in theme settings
Menu links via Drupal menu admin
Agency name and contact info via custom blocks
knownLimitations: Big footer with collapsible sections needs JS
Identifier component (usa-identifier) is separate — add as a custom block in footer_secondary

components.title: Form (Drupal Custom Twig + Form API)
avoidWhen: When a Webform module form suffices
components.agentPrompt: Use Drupal's Form API to build forms. Override form-element.html.twig and input.html.twig in your theme to add usa-form, usa-input, usa-label, usa-button classes. For the form wrapper, override form.html.twig to add usa-form class. Use #attributes to add USWDS classes in your form builder.
components.twigTemplate: form.html.twig, form-element.html.twig, input.html.twig, fieldset.html.twig, textarea.html.twig, select.html.twig in themes/custom/mytheme/templates/
components.preprocessFunction: mytheme_preprocess_form() — add usa-form class. mytheme_preprocess_form_element() — add usa-label, usa-input classes. mytheme_preprocess_input() — map input types to USWDS classes.
knownLimitations: Drupal's Form API generates its own markup — significant template overrides needed
Drupal's error handling uses different markup than USWDS validation patterns
Checkbox and radio groups need fieldset wrapper with usa-fieldset class

components.title: Header (Drupal Theme Region)
components.agentPrompt: The uswds base theme provides header_top, header, primary_menu, and secondary_menu regions. Place menu blocks in primary_menu and secondary_menu. The theme settings page (/admin/appearance/settings/uswds) controls header variant (basic vs extended).
components.twigTemplate: page.html.twig in themes/contrib/uswds/templates/ — look for region-header, region-primary_menu blocks. Override in themes/custom/mytheme/templates/page.html.twig
components.configYaml:
# In theme settings:
# /admin/appearance/settings/uswds
# Set header style: basic or extended
editableAreas: Header variant (basic vs extended) in theme settings
Menu structure via Drupal menu admin
Search block placement in secondary_menu region
knownLimitations: Extended header variant requires specific region block placement
Megamenu requires custom Twig work — not provided by base theme

components.title: Tag, Link, Icon, Pagination (Drupal Custom Twig)
avoidWhen: When a paragraph bundle or theme region already handles the component
components.agentPrompt: These components have no dedicated paragraph bundle. Implement via Twig template overrides: field.html.twig for tags (add usa-tag), link.html.twig for links (add usa-link), menu.html.twig for pagination (add usa-pagination), and use USWDS sprite SVGs for icons.
components.twigTemplate: field--type-list-string.html.twig (for tags), link.html.twig, menu.html.twig (for pagination), field--field-icon.html.twig (for icons)
components.preprocessFunction: mytheme_preprocess_field() — detect tag fields and add usa-tag class. mytheme_preprocess_link() — add usa-link. mytheme_preprocess_menu() — add usa-pagination for pager menus.
editableAreas: Pagination page range and current page
preserveElements: usa-tag, usa-link, usa-icon, usa-pagination class structure
xlink:href to USWDS sprite.svg for icons
rel='external' or download attribute on special links
knownLimitations: USWDS sprite.svg must be in theme assets directory
Pagination via Views pager needs separate template (views-mini-pager.html.twig)
External link detection requires custom logic in preprocess

components.title: Modal (Drupal Paragraph Bundle)
useWhen: Supplemental information that shouldn't need a full page
avoidWhen: Form modals (use Drupal's modal API instead)
Critical content that must always be visible
components.agentPrompt: Enable uswds_paragraph_components_modal submodule. Editor creates a modal paragraph with trigger text, heading, and body content. USWDS JS handles open/close behavior.

fieldDefinitions.field_uswds_modal_trigger: Text for the button that opens the modal
fieldDefinitions.field_uswds_modal_body: Text formatted field for modal content

knownLimitations: Requires USWDS JS for open/close
Not integrated with Drupal's modal API
Content inside modal is not lazy-loaded

components.title: Process List (Drupal Paragraph Bundle)
avoidWhen: Simple bullet lists (use regular list instead)
components.agentPrompt: Enable uswds_paragraph_components_process_list submodule. Create a parent 'uswds_process_list' paragraph, then add 'uswds_process_item' children. Each item has a heading and body.

fieldDefinitions.field_uswds_process_heading: Text field for each step title
fieldDefinitions.field_uswds_process_body: Text formatted field for each step

editableAreas: Template override for custom step numbering
knownLimitations: Process items are child paragraphs
Numbering is automatic via CSS counters

components.title: Search (Drupal Theme Region)
avoidWhen: When search is in a dropdown panel (custom Twig needed)
components.agentPrompt: Place the Drupal search form block in the secondary_menu region. The uswds theme converts Drupal's search form to usa-search markup. For usa-search--small or --big variants, override the template.
components.twigTemplate: search.html.twig or form--search-form.html.twig in themes/contrib/uswds/templates/. Override to change variant (default, big, small).
components.preprocessFunction: mytheme_preprocess_form() — check for search form and add usa-search--small class
editableAreas: Search variant (default, big, small) via template override
knownLimitations: Drupal's search form has extra div wrappers that need CSS normalization
Big/small variants require template override
Search API integration requires additional configuration

components.title: USWDS Drupal Setup
useWhen: Starting a new Drupal + USWDS government site
Setting up the USWDS ecosystem on an existing Drupal site
components.agentPrompt: Run these composer and drush commands in order to install the full USWDS-Drupal stack. Then create a subtheme of uswds and configure USWDS SCSS settings.
drushCommands: composer require drupal/paragraphs drupal/entity_reference_revisions drupal/viewsreference drupal/field_group drupal/twig_tweak
drush pm:enable paragraphs entity_reference_revisions viewsreference field_group twig_tweak

drush pm:enable uswds_paragraph_components_accordion uswds_paragraph_components_alert uswds_paragraph_components_cards uswds_paragraph_components_modal uswds_paragraph_components_process_list uswds_paragraph_components_step_indicator uswds_paragraph_components_summary_box
components.configYaml:
# Create a subtheme:
# Copy themes/custom/uswds/examples/uswds_gulp_subtheme to web/themes/custom/mytheme
# Set as default theme: drush config-set system.theme default mytheme
knownLimitations: USWDS SCSS compilation requires Node.js and gulp
Some paragraph bundles depend on others (Cards Flag depends on Cards)

components.title: Side Navigation (Drupal Theme Region)
components.agentPrompt: The uswds base theme auto-styles any menu block placed in sidebar_first as usa-sidenav. Drupal's menu system provides the hierarchy; the theme converts it to usa-sidenav markup with usa-current on the active trail.
components.twigTemplate: menu.html.twig in themes/contrib/uswds/templates/. The theme provides a usa-sidenav version of the menu template.
components.preprocessFunction: mytheme_preprocess_menu() — add custom classes or modify items
editableAreas: Which menu to place in sidebar_first
Menu depth (how many levels to show)
knownLimitations: Menu structure must be defined in Drupal's menu admin
usa-current is applied based on Drupal's active trail — may not match for views pages
Sub-list expansion requires menu depth > 1

components.title: Step Indicator (Drupal Paragraph Bundle)
useWhen: Showing completion status of a process
components.agentPrompt: Enable uswds_paragraph_components_step_indicator submodule. Create a parent 'uswds_step_indicator' paragraph, then add 'uswds_step_indicator_item' children. Mark one as current.

fieldDefinitions.field_uswds_step_label: Text field for each step label

knownLimitations: Step items are child paragraphs
State management is manual (editor sets complete/current/incomplete)
Not integrated with Drupal's form API multi-step forms

components.title: Summary Box (Drupal Paragraph Bundle)
useWhen: Key takeaways at top of content
avoidWhen: Error summaries (use Drupal form error handling)
components.agentPrompt: Enable uswds_paragraph_components_summary_box submodule. Editor creates a summary box with heading and body text (can include list items).
knownLimitations: Variant (info/warning/error) styling may require custom CSS
Not auto-generated from content — editor must write summary

components.title: Table (Drupal Custom Twig)
components.agentPrompt: Override table.html.twig in your theme to add usa-table class to all Drupal tables. For Views tables, override views-view-table.html.twig. For stacked/borderless/striped variants, add the variant class in preprocess based on a field or view setting.
components.preprocessFunction: mytheme_preprocess_table() — add usa-table class. mytheme_preprocess_views_view_table() — add variant classes.
editableAreas: Responsive wrapper div for scrollable tables
knownLimitations: Drupal Views tables have different template suggestions than content tables
Stacked variant requires data-label attributes on td elements (manual in Twig)
Scrollable variant needs a wrapper div around the table

components.title: Text Input (Drupal Custom Twig)
avoidWhen: When using Webform module (has its own theming)
components.agentPrompt: Override input.html.twig and form-element--type-textfield.html.twig in your theme. Add usa-input class to the input element and usa-label to the label. For error states, add usa-input--error and wrap error message in usa-error-message.
components.preprocessFunction: mytheme_preprocess_input() — add usa-input class. mytheme_preprocess_form_element() — add usa-label class to labels.
editableAreas: Input type attribute (text, email, tel, url, search, password)
knownLimitations: Drupal wraps inputs in form-element divs that need CSS normalization
Error message markup differs between Drupal and USWDS
